description: Fine mineral earth that becomes mouldable with water is 'mueri'. It firms as it dries and can become ceramic when fired. 'muphia' (mud) is earth mixed with water more generally; once heat hardens the clay into 'mueta' (ceramic), the sentence has moved to another material.
articulatory_notes: Closed lips hold voiced nasal 'm' before opening into high-back rounded 'u'. The voice crosses a clear hiatus into stressed front 'e'; a tap or brief trill releases the final high-front 'i'.
usage_notes: Bare 'mueri' treats clay as material. A local name can supply its composition and working range when a potter needs the distinction.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A potter reads clay through the hand: water, pressure, drying, and fire each change what the material will bear. That knowledge belongs to the particular clay and kiln.

semantic_domains.creation: a mouldable material used in pottery and building
semantic_domains.nature: fine mineral earth found in particular soils and deposits
semantic_domains.physical: mineral particles that become plastic when mixed with water
